# Migration Plan: VSTest → Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP)

## Problem Statement

Migrate 5 MSTest test projects (MSTest 3.8.0, .NET 8 SDK) from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform. CI uses Azure DevOps with VSTest@3, TRX reporting, and code coverage.

## Key Constraints

- .NET 8 SDK → requires `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` property and `--` separator for MTP args
- MSTest 3.8.0 ≥ 3.2.0 → no MSTest version upgrade needed
- All 5 projects must migrate together (mixed VSTest/MTP is unsupported)

---

## Phase 1: Baseline & Preparation

1. Run `dotnet test` on all 5 projects and record pass/fail counts
2. Identify test project naming convention (e.g., `*.Tests`) for Directory.Build.props conditioning
3. Confirm no project uses `MSTest.Sdk` (they use NuGet packages per the description)

## Phase 2: Directory.Build.props Setup

Add/update `Directory.Build.props` at the solution root:

```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
    <!-- Enable MTP runner for MSTest -->
    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>

    <!-- Enable dotnet test integration on .NET 8/9 SDK -->
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

> Adjust the `EndsWith` condition to match your actual naming convention.

## Phase 3: Add MTP Extension Packages

Add to each test project (or centrally via `Directory.Packages.props` if using Central Package Management):

```xml
<!-- TRX reporting (replaces --logger trx) -->
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.TrxReport" Version="1.6.2" />

<!-- Code coverage (replaces --collect "Code Coverage") -->
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CodeCoverage" Version="17.13.0" />
```

## Phase 4: Update Azure DevOps Pipeline

Replace the `VSTest@3` task with `DotNetCoreCLI@2`:

```yaml
# BEFORE
- task: VSTest@3
  inputs:
    testAssemblyVer2: '**/*Tests.dll'
    runSettingsFile: 'test.runsettings'
    codeCoverageEnabled: true

# AFTER
- task: DotNetCoreCLI@2
  displayName: 'Run Tests'
  inputs:
    command: 'test'
    projects: '**/*Tests.csproj'
    arguments: '--no-build --configuration Release -- --report-trx --results-directory $(Agent.TempDirectory) --coverage --coverage-output-format cobertura'

- task: PublishTestResults@2
  displayName: 'Publish Test Results'
  inputs:
    testResultsFormat: 'VSTest'
    testResultsFiles: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)/**/*.trx'
  condition: always()

- task: PublishCodeCoverageResults@2
  displayName: 'Publish Code Coverage'
  inputs:
    summaryFileLocation: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)/**/coverage.cobertura.xml'
  condition: always()
```

### Argument translation summary

| Old (VSTest) | New (MTP via `--` separator) |
|---|---|
| `--logger trx` | `-- --report-trx` |
| `--collect "Code Coverage"` | `-- --coverage --coverage-output-format cobertura` |
| `--filter "expr"` | `-- --filter "expr"` (same syntax for MSTest) |
| `--settings file.runsettings` | `-- --settings file.runsettings` |
| `--blame-hang --blame-hang-timeout 5m` | `-- --hangdump --hangdump-timeout 5m` (+ NuGet pkg) |

> **Critical**: On .NET 8/9 SDK, MTP arguments MUST go after `--`.

## Phase 5: Handle Exit Code 8 (Zero Tests Discovered)

MTP returns exit code 8 when no tests are discovered (VSTest silently succeeds). To preserve current behavior, add to `Directory.Build.props`:

```xml
<PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
  <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>$(TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments) --ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>
</PropertyGroup>
```

Or handle in CI with environment variable: `TESTINGPLATFORM_EXITCODE_IGNORE=8`.

## Phase 6: Remove VSTest-Only Packages (after validation)

Remove from each test project:
- `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` (17.13.0) — not needed for MTP
- `MSTest.TestAdapter` (3.8.0) — not needed; `EnableMSTestRunner` handles discovery

Keep:
- `MSTest.TestFramework` (3.8.0) — still needed for test APIs

## Phase 7: Verify

1. `dotnet build` — zero errors
2. `dotnet test` — all tests pass, counts match Phase 1 baseline
3. Run executable directly: `./bin/Debug/net8.0/MyProject.Tests.exe --list-tests`
4. CI pipeline produces TRX + coverage artifacts
5. Visual Studio Test Explorer discovers tests

---

## Notes & Pitfalls

- **Do NOT mix VSTest and MTP** — migrate all 5 projects in one commit
- **`--` separator is mandatory** on .NET 8/9 for MTP args
- **`IsTestProject` condition won't work** in Directory.Build.props — use name-based condition
- **If upgrading to .NET 10 later**: replace `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` with `global.json` `"test": { "runner": "Microsoft.Testing.Platform" }` and drop the `--` separator

## References

- [Migrate from VSTest to MTP](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/core/testing/migrating-vstest-microsoft-testing-platform)
- [MTP CLI options](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/core/testing/microsoft-testing-platform-cli-options)
- [MTP extensions](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/core/testing/unit-testing-platform-extensions)

# Migration Plan: VSTest → Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP)

## Problem Statement

Migrate 5 MSTest test projects (MSTest 3.8.0, .NET 8 SDK) from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform. CI uses Azure DevOps with VSTest@3, TRX reporting, and code coverage.

## Key Constraints

- .NET 8 SDK → requires `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` property and `--` separator for MTP args
- MSTest 3.8.0 ≥ 3.2.0 → no MSTest version upgrade needed
- All 5 projects must migrate together (mixed VSTest/MTP is unsupported)

---

## Phase 1: Baseline & Preparation

1. Run `dotnet test` on all 5 projects and record pass/fail counts
2. Identify test project naming convention (e.g., `*.Tests`) for Directory.Build.props conditioning
3. Confirm no project uses `MSTest.Sdk` (they use NuGet packages per the description)

## Phase 2: Directory.Build.props Setup

Add/update `Directory.Build.props` at the solution root:

```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
    <!-- Enable MTP runner for MSTest -->
    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>

    <!-- Enable dotnet test integration on .NET 8/9 SDK -->
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

> Adjust the `EndsWith` condition to match your actual naming convention.

## Phase 3: Add MTP Extension Packages

Add to each test project (or centrally via `Directory.Packages.props` if using Central Package Management):

```xml
<!-- TRX reporting (replaces --logger trx) -->
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.TrxReport" Version="1.6.2" />

<!-- Code coverage (replaces --collect "Code Coverage") -->
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CodeCoverage" Version="17.13.0" />
```

## Phase 4: Update Azure DevOps Pipeline

Replace the `VSTest@3` task with `DotNetCoreCLI@2`:

```yaml
# BEFORE
- task: VSTest@3
  inputs:
    testAssemblyVer2: '**/*Tests.dll'
    runSettingsFile: 'test.runsettings'
    codeCoverageEnabled: true

# AFTER
- task: DotNetCoreCLI@2
  displayName: 'Run Tests'
  inputs:
    command: 'test'
    projects: '**/*Tests.csproj'
    arguments: '--no-build --configuration Release -- --report-trx --results-directory $(Agent.TempDirectory) --coverage --coverage-output-format cobertura'

- task: PublishTestResults@2
  displayName: 'Publish Test Results'
  inputs:
    testResultsFormat: 'VSTest'
    testResultsFiles: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)/**/*.trx'
  condition: always()

- task: PublishCodeCoverageResults@2
  displayName: 'Publish Code Coverage'
  inputs:
    summaryFileLocation: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)/**/coverage.cobertura.xml'
  condition: always()
```

### Argument translation summary

| Old (VSTest) | New (MTP via `--` separator) |
|---|---|
| `--logger trx` | `-- --report-trx` |
| `--collect "Code Coverage"` | `-- --coverage --coverage-output-format cobertura` |
| `--filter "expr"` | `-- --filter "expr"` (same syntax for MSTest) |
| `--settings file.runsettings` | `-- --settings file.runsettings` |
| `--blame-hang --blame-hang-timeout 5m` | `-- --hangdump --hangdump-timeout 5m` (+ NuGet pkg) |

> **Critical**: On .NET 8/9 SDK, MTP arguments MUST go after `--`.

## Phase 5: Handle Exit Code 8 (Zero Tests Discovered)

MTP returns exit code 8 when no tests are discovered (VSTest silently succeeds). To preserve current behavior, add to `Directory.Build.props`:

```xml
<PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
  <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>$(TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments) --ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>
</PropertyGroup>
```

Or handle in CI with environment variable: `TESTINGPLATFORM_EXITCODE_IGNORE=8`.

## Phase 6: Remove VSTest-Only Packages (after validation)

Remove from each test project:
- `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` (17.13.0) — not needed for MTP
- `MSTest.TestAdapter` (3.8.0) — not needed; `EnableMSTestRunner` handles discovery

Keep:
- `MSTest.TestFramework` (3.8.0) — still needed for test APIs

## Phase 7: Verify

1. `dotnet build` — zero errors
2. `dotnet test` — all tests pass, counts match Phase 1 baseline
3. Run executable directly: `./bin/Debug/net8.0/MyProject.Tests.exe --list-tests`
4. CI pipeline produces TRX + coverage artifacts
5. Visual Studio Test Explorer discovers tests

---

## Notes & Pitfalls

- **Do NOT mix VSTest and MTP** — migrate all 5 projects in one commit
- **`--` separator is mandatory** on .NET 8/9 for MTP args
- **`IsTestProject` condition won't work** in Directory.Build.props — use name-based condition
- **If upgrading to .NET 10 later**: replace `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` with `global.json` `"test": { "runner": "Microsoft.Testing.Platform" }` and drop the `--` separator

## References

- [Migrate from VSTest to MTP](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/core/testing/migrating-vstest-microsoft-testing-platform)
- [MTP CLI options](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/core/testing/microsoft-testing-platform-cli-options)
- [MTP extensions](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/core/testing/unit-testing-platform-extensions)
